name: Squarespace API
what_it_does: Provides programmatic access to Squarespace store data including products, orders, inventory, and customer information for commerce and content automation.
use_cases: Sync Squarespace product catalog with an external PIM or ERP system
Pull order data into a fulfillment or logistics workflow
Monitor inventory levels and trigger restocking alerts
Extract customer purchase history for CRM enrichment
Automate discount and promotion management across a store
not_for: Building a fully custom storefront outside of Squarespace (locked to Squarespace templates)
High-volume transactional platforms needing sub-100ms latency at scale
Accessing Squarespace website builder or page content programmatically (limited CMS API)
best_when: You already host a store on Squarespace and need to integrate it with external fulfillment, ERP, or analytics systems.
avoid_when: You need a headless commerce solution with full front-end flexibility or need APIs beyond Squarespace's commerce scope.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for third-party app integrations; API keys for personal/internal access. Scopes are coarse-grained per resource (orders, inventory, products).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; API access requires an active Squarespace Commerce plan.
pricing.paid_tiers: Commerce Basic (~$28/mo) and Commerce Advanced (~$52/mo) unlock API access; Business plan has limited commerce features.
pricing.notes: API access is gated to paying Squarespace subscribers; no sandbox environment without a plan.

performance.rate_limits: Approximately 1,000 requests per hour per store; limits are not well-documented in official docs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses return HTTP status codes with brief JSON messages; root cause details are often vague and require trial-and-error debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order and product endpoints do not expose idempotency keys; duplicate webhook delivery is possible and agents must deduplicate manually.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are undocumented and enforced inconsistently — agents must implement conservative backoff strategies
Webhooks can deliver duplicate events; agents must implement idempotent event processing using order/product IDs
No sandbox environment; testing requires a live paid Squarespace store
OAuth token refresh flow is poorly documented and tokens expire without clear expiration signals
Inventory updates via API do not immediately reflect on storefront due to eventual consistency delays

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 enforced over TLS; scopes are coarse-grained (per resource area, not per action), limiting least-privilege access for agents.
